Kole

Cybersecurity / IT

I help companies manage thier IT enviornment by setting up network infrastructure, building security stacks, monitoring, and fixing probelms as they appear.
I plan to specicialize on the security side of things. I'm not toally sure what niche within that field yet, I really do love all of it, but currently I'm very focused on security engineering and blue team activities.

Quick Info

Degree Bachelors in Computer Science

Hello and Welcome

This is my portfolio website. I created this because I thought it would be cool to do, because I'm tired of having my notes in so many different places. But most importantly, I created it becasue in hopes that it can be of use to others. One of the first things that drew me into IT/cybersecurity was seeing the amount of free tooling and information and the time people put into it. Now that I have some time in the field, I want to begin contributing.

View Write-Ups View Resume

Featured Projects

Home Network/Lab Setup
Networking / Infrastrcture / Sys Admin
I created a virtualized lab enviornment for myself using Virutal Box. In recent months, I upgraded to a dedicated Proxmox server and added a firewall and a lot of additional infrastructure to my actual home network. The repo was made before I upgraded so it does not reflect by current infrastrcuture, but still contains a lot of great information, in my opinion. I kind of used this respository to make a huge set of documentation about all of the things I learned. The main focus is Active Directory both management, exploitation, and defense.
Repository
Wazuh + YARA-X Active Response
Detection / Security / Automation
Automated malware scanning + quarantine tooling via Yara-X integrated into Wazuh Active Response. Designed for low false positives, operational logging, and better security. Uses Yara-X as Yara is now in maintenace mode. I did this to increase security the linux machines on my network as they do not feature built in anti-virus.
Coming Soon...
Securing Your Active Directory Enviornment
Security / Active Directory
Active Directory is the backbone of most enterprise networks, and I also just think its super cool. I'm working to make a defintive guide for standing up Wazuh for an Active Directory enviornment. I will be integrating a lot of 3rd party tools into Wazuh and our Windows endpoints themselves. This will be a full Active Directory security guide, going over necessary base security configurations(GPOs), of course standing up Wazuh and building it out significantly, potentially standing up other security tools I find useful, and some network secuirty baselines too. This will be security baselines, focusing mainly on things that should be done on every single Active Directory network.
Coming Soon...
